summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, regulation.

articles.title: How Visa targets B2B payments
articles.description: The card network is taking aim at verticals to expand in business-to-business payments, including with services for trucking fleet operators.

articles.title: Visa-Mastercard fund will report claims status
articles.description: Merchants will be able to monitor the status of claims under a quarterly reporting system for their $5.5 billion settlement with Visa and Mastercard.

articles.title: From delayed invoices to improved cash flow: The case for more flexible payments.
articles.description: For small and mid-market business owners, cash flow doesn’t feel like a line on a spreadsheet.

articles.title: The revenue leak every retail executive is ignoring
articles.description: Your fraud system is making revenue decisions, but is anyone measuring the cost?

articles.title: States aim to keep stablecoin purview
articles.description: A group representing state bank supervisors is concerned about preserving sovereignty over the digital assets.

articles.title: JPMorgan taps AI to process checks
articles.description: With paper checks remaining a popular form of payment, JPMorgan Chase is using AI and robotics to streamline its lockbox processing.

articles.title: Ramp debuts accounting product, raises additional $750M
articles.description: The new product marks the startup’s entry into accounting, coming as Ramp raised more capital to boost its AI work.

articles.title: Colorado interchange fee law vetoed
articles.description: The state’s governor shot down a bill aimed at curbing interchange fees on card transactions even though members of his own party passed the legislation.
